hypothesis: Compiling the regex pattern at module level instead of per-call will eliminate pattern compilation overhead on each tokenize() invocation.
change_summary: Move re.compile() to module level as _TOKEN_PATTERN; replace re.split(pattern, text) with _TOKEN_PATTERN.split(text)
reason: Median improved by 17.7% (12.4 → 10.2 ms). Tests pass. Change is safe and reversible.

hypothesis: Replacing the explicit loop in process_batch() with a list comprehension using the walrus operator will eliminate the overhead of a separate normalize() call per token, since we currently call normalize() once to check and once implicitly via append.
change_summary: Replace for-loop + normalize() call in process_batch with [n for t in tokens if (n := t.strip().lower())]
reason: Median improved by 3.9% (10.2 → 9.8 ms). Walrus pattern is idiomatic Python 3.8+. Tests pass.
